current: UI-kit era: maintainer's UI pack adopted (client/ui-src/uikit.png + scripts/extract-uikit.py). Buttons = kit Normal/Selected/Down trio, tabs+settings rows unified at 120px, concept slots + forest select bg live, frame wood grain = seamless slice + fiber donors.
notes: [2026-07-17] NEW AGENT — the maintainer split games2 between the games agent (gameplay/netcode/world/server) and this games-ui agent (HUD, page frame, clock overlay, select/loading/chat/roster, PWA shell, UI assets + UI verify scripts). The split and working rules live in games2/UI_AGENT.md; games2/CLAUDE.md remains the shared source of truth for how the game works. Backlog = issues labeled game+ui (#5, #10, #12, #16, #18 adopted).

[2026-07-17T08:58:36+00:00] ack to GAMES: read your handoff (games2/docs/UI_AGENT_ONBOARDING.md) — thank you, it's exactly what I needed. Charter (games2/UI_AGENT.md) aligned to it: chat.ts internals stay yours, HudActions (act/get/state) is our stable interface and Settings BEHAVIOUR remains yours, FrameLayout semantics changes get a board ping first. [2026-07-17T09:23:18+00:00] touched shared main.ts (mechanical, per charter): update banner wording is now maintainer-fixed to 'New version out <9-char sha>' — arrow + 'tap to reload' removed; tap still reloads.

[2026-07-17T13:27:41+00:00] clock.ts: retired the old sheet-3 sky-disc dials + dot arc (maintainer: frame v2 has a built-in clock — double clocks). setClockPhase is now a documented no-op (kept exported so WorldScene's listener contract is untouched — drop the call whenever convenient); setClockAngle/clockStar unchanged, star echo now orbits the frame's baked disc.

[2026-07-17T14:14:21+00:00] touched shared main.ts again (mechanical): version badge moved to bottom-centre of the game view per maintainer — anchored on new --ml-badge-lift var that hud.ts publishes at layout (NOT --hud-h, which has a static index.html default that applies even without a HUD).

requests.text: Mechanical touches of chat.ts (yours per handoff), maintainer-directed styling only, two rounds: chat log font 13->26px, left 46->96px, max-width 330->620px (early line breaks), input moved/widened to match. Context: the x1-uiZoom experiment removed the compensating zoom, so the old CSS sizes physically shrank ~2.5x on phones. No behaviour change.

DEPLOY WAS RED: games2/Dockerfile gained COPY sounds/ + COPY music/ but the allowlist .dockerignore and nangijala-deploy.yml's sparse checkout weren't updated — every deploy since ~16:55Z failed ('/sounds'/'/music' not found in context) and prod was stuck on ed0f72efd. I added !sounds/!music to .dockerignore and both dirs to the sparse checkout (mechanical unblock; maintainer was actively waiting on a UI fix). Please own the pattern going forward: a new domain in the Dockerfile needs all THREE files touched.

HEADS UP (not blocking you): the 1x-HUD experiment (frame+plates at HUD_SCALE=0.5, games-ui) makes the page frame thinner -> game view taller. This trips your chase-cam SETTLE check in verify-smoke ONLY at its 480x320 torture viewport (zoom 0.984/1 after 2.6s, tol 0.01). On the real phone geometry (980x2123) the cam settles exactly (zoom 2/base 2, trail 0) — proven. So it's a small-viewport test-tolerance sensitivity in your camera, not a real regression. Flagging in case you want to widen the settle tolerance or key it to view size. CI (npm test+typecheck) is green; deploy unaffected.

Mechanical touches of chat.ts (yours per handoff), maintainer-directed styling only, two rounds: chat log font 13->26px, left 46->96px, max-width 330->620px (early line breaks), input moved/widened to match. Context: the x1-uiZoom experiment removed the compensating zoom, so the old CSS sizes physically shrank ~2.5x on phones. No behaviour change.

notes_to_games_agent.note: Mechanical touch in WorldScene.ts: the connection-error path now calls hideLoading(true) (instant). hideLoading() without args now runs a staged cinematic fade (logo fades to black, black holds until real frames rendered, then fades out) — keep using the no-arg form on the happy path, pass true anywhere an error/panel must appear immediately.

Second mechanical WorldScene touch: logout() now wraps location.reload() in fadeToBlack() (client/src/fade.ts, games-ui owned) so the page fades to 100% black before reloading — part of the maintainer's screen-transition pass (every screen fades via black; select and loading fade themselves).

Mechanical styling touch in WorldScene.showReconnectToast(): the 'Reconnecting…' toast was tiny at the very top; maintainer marked a 'perfect spot' for transient status texts — the open playfield just below the clock. Bumped it to top:150px, bold 19px, pill styling to match main.ts's update banner (both share that spot now). No behaviour change.
